task_updates.executor_notes: Exported `buildSummary` and extended `SelectedMediaClip` with optional `generationId`. Timeline clip selection still omits the field by default, and `npx tsc --noEmit` passed.

Added a distinct multi-generation drag MIME type, setter/getter helpers, and `getDragType()` detection that checks multi-generation payloads before single-generation payloads. Verified with the full drag/drop module test file and repository typecheck.
Extended `SelectedClipPayload` with optional `generation_id`, preserving existing clip payload consumers. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed after the type change.

Added `GallerySelectionProvider` with filtered selection state, per-item and batch selection actions, derived `SelectedMediaClip[]`, and summary generation via `buildSummary`. Verified with the full `GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx` file plus repository typecheck.

Updated `useExternalDrop` to accept `generation-multi` drags, check multi-generation payloads before single-generation payloads, and place dropped assets sequentially on the timeline while preserving one created track for multi-drop new-track inserts. Video drops now propagate duration metadata into the registry before placement when available. Verified with the full `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` file plus repository typecheck.

Split selected-clip normalization into a reusable helper so gallery attachments with `generation_id` synthesize a stable `clip_id` instead of being dropped. `resolveClipGenerationIds()` now falls back to `generation_id` when no timeline clip registry lookup succeeds. Verified with dedicated edge test files for `selectedClips` and `tools/clips`, plus repository typecheck.

Extended the agent attachment type with optional `generationId` and updated the request payload builder to emit `generation_id` only when an attachment provides one. Verified with the full `useAgentSession.test.tsx` file plus repository typecheck.

Mounted `GallerySelectionProvider` in the composed app provider tree immediately after `PanesProvider`, keeping it available to descendants under both the layout-level pane tree and the video-editor subtree. Verified with the full `AppProviders.test.tsx` file plus repository typecheck.

Updated `AgentChatMessage` so attachment summaries render whenever a turn includes attachments, which keeps gallery-origin attachments visible in chat history when they carry `generationId`. Verified with the full `AgentChatMessage.test.tsx` file plus repository typecheck.

Wired single-click and modifier-assisted gallery selection through `GenerationsPaneGallery`, added `useModifierKeys`, and passed `selectedIds` plus `enableSingleClick` into the gallery surface. Verified with the full `GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx`, `useModifierKeys.test.tsx`, `useMediaGalleryViewInteractions.test.ts`, and `useItemInteraction.test.ts` modules plus repository typecheck.

Added `useLassoSelection` and hooked it into the generations pane surface, including background-only start detection, append-on-modifier behavior, and a visible selection rectangle overlay. Verified with the full `useLassoSelection.test.tsx` and `GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx` modules plus repository typecheck.

Merged timeline and gallery selections in `AgentChat`, deduplicated attachments by URL while preferring gallery metadata when it adds `generationId`, surfaced the combined attachment summary in the chat UI, and cleared gallery selection after a successful send. Verified with the full `AgentChat.test.tsx` module plus repository typecheck.

Threaded gallery selection state through `MediaGallery` into `MediaGalleryGrid`, added optional `isSelected` support on `MediaGalleryItem`, and applied the timeline-style `ring-2 ring-sky-400` highlight without changing callers that omit selection state. Verified with the full `MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, `MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx`, `useMediaGalleryViewInteractions.test.ts`, `useItemInteraction.test.ts`, and `GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx` modules plus repository typecheck.

Updated `MediaGalleryItem` drag initiation to emit a multi-generation payload when the dragged item belongs to a multi-selection, while preserving the existing single-item drag payload for single selections and non-selected drags. Added a count badge to the drag preview for multi-item drags and verified the source/drop contract with the full `dragDrop.test.ts`, `MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, `MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx`, `useItemInteraction.test.ts`, and `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` modules plus repository typecheck.

Ran the final feature verification bundle covering `GallerySelectionContext`, `AppProviders`, `GenerationsPaneGallery`, `useModifierKeys`, `useLassoSelection`, `MediaGalleryGrid`, `useMediaGalleryViewInteractions`, `MediaGalleryItem` behavior, `useItemInteraction`, drag/drop utilities, `useExternalDrop`, `useAgentSession`, `AgentChat`, and `AgentChatMessage`, then confirmed `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. There is no dedicated `useSelectedMediaClips` test module in the repository, so its current behavior was verified through the existing `AgentChat.test.tsx` consumer coverage. Also wrote and ran a temporary `GallerySelectionContext` verification test exercising `selectGalleryItem`, `selectGalleryItems`, and `clearGallerySelection`, then deleted it.
commands_run: npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts .tmp-gallery-selection-verification.test.tsx src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx src/app/providers/AppProviders.test.tsx src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.test.tsx src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/hooks/useModifierKeys.test.tsx src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/hooks/useLassoSelection.test.tsx src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/__tests__/MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryViewInteractions.test.ts src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.behavior.test.tsx src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useItemInteraction.test.ts src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChatMessage.test.tsx

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The new `generationId` field is optional, so timeline clip selections continue to produce the same shape unless a gallery-backed selection sets it later.
Multi-generation drags use a separate `application/x-generation-multi` MIME type and text prefix, and `getDragType()` checks that path first so single and multi drags remain distinguishable.
The new `generation_id` field is optional on `SelectedClipPayload`, so existing callers do not need to provide it and current type consumers continue to compile.
The gallery selection normalization rejects anything whose type does not include `image` or `video`, so effects and music never enter `selectedGalleryIds`.

The timeline drop handler now checks `getMultiGenerationDropData()` before `getGenerationDropData()`, and multi-drop placement advances time offsets sequentially using 5 seconds for images and video duration metadata when available.

Selected clip normalization now accepts gallery payloads that only provide `generation_id` by synthesizing `clip_id` values like `gallery-gen-123`, and `resolveClipGenerationIds()` falls back to that `generation_id` whenever the timeline clip lookup misses.

The frontend `selected_clips` payload uses a conditional object spread, so `generation_id` is only sent for attachments that actually have `generationId`; the `useAgentSession` test asserts the omitted-property case.

The provider tree now inserts `GallerySelectionProvider` directly after `PanesProvider`, and the `AppProviders` test confirms descendants rendered under the composed tree can consume the gallery-selection context.

Attachment summaries no longer depend on the turn being a user turn, so gallery-style attachments that carry `generationId` still render correctly in chat history; the `AgentChatMessage` test covers that path.

Single click now replaces the gallery selection, while shift/cmd/ctrl clicks pass `{ toggle: true }` through the shared selection context. The `useModifierKeys` hook test also verifies listener registration and cleanup on unmount.

The lasso hook exits immediately when the pointer starts inside an element marked with `data-gallery-item-id`, so drag-selection only begins from background gaps. Its append flag is derived from shift/meta/ctrl on mousedown, and the lasso test covers the additive case.

AgentChat now merges timeline and gallery clips by URL before sending, prefers the gallery-backed attachment when that is the one carrying `generationId`, and clears gallery selection only after `sendMessage.mutateAsync(...)` resolves. The `AgentChat` test asserts both the deduplicated payload and the post-send clear.

Selected gallery items now render with the same `ring-2 ring-sky-400 rounded-lg` treatment, and the grid normalizes missing `selectedIds` to `false` so callers that omit selection state continue to render identically. The grid and item behavior tests cover both the selected and unselected paths.

The drag handler now switches to `setMultiGenerationDragData(...)` only when the dragged item is itself selected and there is more than one selected item in the passed `selectedItems` prop. Otherwise it falls back to the existing single-item `setGenerationDragData(...)` path exactly; the `MediaGalleryItem` behavior test covers that fallback branch.

All existing targeted verification modules passed in one full run, and `npx tsc --noEmit` completed without type errors. The repository does not contain a dedicated `useSelectedMediaClips` test file, so that behavior was validated via the existing `AgentChat` consumer test alongside the rest of the final suite.
